ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (Hafnium) — IIS w3wp.exe spawning shell/recon child on Exchange (ProxyLogon post-exploitation)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Hafnium / Operation Exchange Marauder post-exploit fingerprint: an Exchange IIS app-pool worker (w3wp.exe) spawning interactive shells, recon LOLBins, or credential-dump tooling. This is the execution side of the China Chopper / ASPX web shell that Hafnium drops via the ProxyLogon chain (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) and subsequent ProxyShell-style Exchange CVEs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","csc.exe","cvtres.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search NOT (process_name IN ("csc.exe","cvtres.exe") AND parent_process_path="*\\Microsoft\\Exchange Server\\*\\bin\\*") | eval is_recon=if(match(process_name,"(?i)^(whoami|systeminfo|ipconfig|tasklist|quser|nltest|hostname|net|net1)\\.exe$"),1,0) | eval is_creddump=if(match(process,"(?i)(procdump|lsass|sekurlsa|comsvcs\\.dll|MiniDump|ntds\\.dit)"),1,0) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / Hafnium — Exchange w3wp.exe child-of-webshell pattern
let ExchangeLOLBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","csc.exe","cvtres.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
// scope to Exchange app-pools — keeps SharePoint / other IIS hosts out
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool","MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool","MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool","MSExchangePowerShellAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool")
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Microsoft\Exchange Server\"
| where FileName in~ (ExchangeLOLBins)
// Strip Exchange CU / SU patching activity — csc/cvtres only when launched from Exchange bin
| where not(FileName in~ ("csc.exe","cvtres.exe") and InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\" and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has "Temporary ASP.NET Files")
| extend IsCredDump = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","sekurlsa","comsvcs.dll, MiniDump","ntds.dit","MiniDump")
| extend IsRecon = FileName in~ ("whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","net.exe","net1.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ChildImage = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsRecon, IsCredDump, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / Hafnium — Exchange w3wp.exe child-of-webshell (SecurityEvent EID 4688)
let ExchangeLOLBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","csc.exe","cvtres.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                  // process create
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
// Exchange role pin — Exchange installs into '\Microsoft\Exchange Server\' under Program Files
| where ParentProcessName has @"\Exchange Server\" or Computer has_any ("EXCH","MAIL","CAS","MBX")
| extend ChildBin = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(ChildBin) in (ExchangeLOLBins)
// strip Exchange patching csc/cvtres recompile noise
| where not(tolower(ChildBin) in ("csc.exe","cvtres.exe") and CommandLine has "Temporary ASP.NET Files")
| extend IsCredDump = CommandLine has_any ("lsass","sekurlsa","comsvcs.dll, MiniDump","ntds.dit","MiniDump")
| extend IsRecon = tolower(ChildBin) in ("whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","net.exe","net1.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain,
          ChildBin, CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
          IsRecon, IsCredDump, NewProcessName, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (Hafnium) — IIS w3wp.exe Child Process on Exchange (ProxyLogon Post-Exploit)
id: 7a4f1c8d-3e2b-4d6c-9a1f-c8e2b5d4f3a1
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned from w3wp.exe inside Microsoft Exchange app pools — the canonical Hafnium / Operation Exchange Marauder fingerprint where China Chopper / custom ASPX web shells (dropped via the ProxyLogon chain CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 and ProxyShell variants) execute LOLBins under the IIS app-pool identity (NETWORK SERVICE / SYSTEM).
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2021/03/03/mitigate-microsoft-exchange-server-vulnerabilities
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - detection.emerging_threats
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_w3wp:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  exchange_context:
    - ParentCommandLine|contains:
        - 'MSExchangeOWAAppPool'
        - 'MSExchangeECPAppPool'
        - 'MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool'
        - 'MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool'
        - 'MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool'
        - 'MSExchangePowerShellAppPool'
        - 'MSExchangeServicesAppPool'
    - ParentImage|contains: '\Microsoft\Exchange Server\'
  suspicious_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  filter_aspnet_recompile:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\csc.exe'
      - '\cvtres.exe'
    ParentImage|contains: '\Exchange Server\'
    CommandLine|contains: 'Temporary ASP.NET Files'
  condition: parent_w3wp and exchange_context and suspicious_child and not filter_aspnet_recompile
falsepositives:
  - Microsoft Exchange CU / Security Update install (csc.exe / cvtres.exe recompiling ASPX) — excluded via filter
  - Exchange admin running diagnostic scripts from inside the IIS console (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon / Hafnium (Microsoft cluster, originally ZIRCONIUM-adjacent) is the named CN-state actor behind Operation Exchange Marauder — the campaign that burned ProxyLogon as a zero-day in early 2021 and has since reused on-prem Exchange CVEs (ProxyShell, OWASSRF) as its preferred initial-access surface. Their published TTP chain is almost identical every time: unauth SSRF to /owa/auth, write a

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (Hafnium) — ASPX web shell file-write into Exchange OAB/OWA/ECP virtual directories
ucs.description: Detects the file-system artifact of Hafnium's ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26858/27065 arbitrary-file-write primitive) and ProxyShell webshell drop: an .aspx/.ashx/.asmx file written into an Exchange auth or OAB path by w3wp.exe, UMWorkerProcess.exe, or an interactive shell. Anchors the detection on the canonical filesystem locations Hafnium uses, not on shell content.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="created" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\autodiscover\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V14\\ClientAccess\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\OAB\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where match(process_name, "(?i)^(w3wp|UMWorkerProcess|cmd|powershell|pwsh)\\.exe$") | where NOT match(file_name, "(?i)^(errorFE|expiredpassword|frownyface|logoff|logon|signout|usrlogon|exporttool|owaauth)\\.aspx$") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / Hafnium — ASPX webshell drop in Exchange auth/OAB paths
let ExchangeShellPaths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\autodiscover\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\rpc\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\OAB\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\"]);
let KnownExchangeAspx = dynamic([
    "errorFE.aspx","expiredpassword.aspx","frownyface.aspx","logoff.aspx","logon.aspx",
    "signout.aspx","usrlogon.aspx","exporttool.aspx","owaauth.aspx","automate.aspx"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any (ExchangeShellPaths)
// strip the canonical Exchange-shipped auth pages (a Hafnium shell named owaauth.aspx still wins because of the path + creator)
| where not(FileName in~ (KnownExchangeAspx) and InitiatingProcessAccountName =~ "system" and InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          DroppedFile = FileName,
          DropPath = FolderPath,
          SHA256,
          FileSize,
          DroppedBy = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DropperAccount = strcat(InitiatingProcessAccountDomain, "\\", InitiatingProcessAccountName)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / Hafnium — ASPX webshell drop in Exchange auth/OAB paths (ASIM)
let ExchangeShellPaths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\autodiscover\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\rpc\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\OAB\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\"]);
let KnownExchangeAspx = dynamic([
    "errorFE.aspx","expiredpassword.aspx","frownyface.aspx","logoff.aspx","logon.aspx",
    "signout.aspx","usrlogon.aspx","exporttool.aspx","owaauth.aspx","automate.aspx"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where TargetFilePath has_any (ExchangeShellPaths)
| where not(TargetFileName in~ (KnownExchangeAspx) and ActorUsername in~ ("SYSTEM","NETWORK SERVICE") and ActingProcessName has @"\Exchange Server\")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
          DroppedFile = TargetFileName,
          DropPath = TargetFilePath,
          TargetFileSHA256,
          TargetFileSize,
          DroppedBy = ActingProcessName,
          DropperCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
          DropperAccount = ActorUsername
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (Hafnium) — ASPX Web Shell File Write into Exchange Virtual Directory
id: 9c2a5e1f-6b4d-4a7e-8f3c-2d9b1a4f5c6e
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of .aspx/.ashx/.asmx files inside Microsoft Exchange OWA/ECP/Autodiscover/OAB virtual directories by w3wp.exe, UMWorkerProcess.exe, or interactive shells — the file-system artifact of Hafnium's ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26858 / 27065 post-auth arbitrary-file-write) and ProxyShell drop chain. Hafnium consistently writes China Chopper / custom ASPX shells into auth/ subfolders so the shell URL inherits the Exchange virtual-directory authentication context.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.volexity.com/blog/2021/03/02/active-exploitation-of-microsoft-exchange-zero-day-vulnerabilities/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2021/03/03/mitigate-microsoft-exchange-server-vulnerabilities
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036.005
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asmx'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\autodiscover\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\rpc\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\OAB\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
  selection_creator:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\UMWorkerProcess.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  filter_legit:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\errorFE.aspx'
      - '\expiredpassword.aspx'
      - '\frownyface.aspx'
      - '\logoff.aspx'
      - '\logon.aspx'
      - '\signout.aspx'
      - '\usrlogon.aspx'
      - '\exporttool.aspx'
      - '\owaauth.aspx'
    Image|contains: '\Exchange Server\'
  condition: selection_ext and selection_path and selection_creator and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Microsoft Exchange Cumulative Update / Security Update installation (legitimately replaces shipped .aspx pages — filtered)
  - Third-party Exchange transport agent installers writing into OAB
level: high
ucs.rationale: Volexity, Microsoft MSTIC, and CISA AA21-062A all anchor Hafnium attribution on the same disk artifact: aspx web shells dropped into Exchange auth/ and OAB virtual directories (most infamously '/owa/auth/Current/themes/resources/' and '/aspnet_client/'). Hafnium picks these paths because the parent IIS auth handler implicitly auths the shell URL, and because the OAB / autodiscover endpoints were t
